title: Dendranthema yoshinaganthum
intro: Dendranthema yoshinaganthum, also known as Japanese daisy or Yoshino daisy, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family, native to Japan and East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help relieve congestion and soothe the respiratory tract. The bioactive constituents of Dendranthema yoshinaganthum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and other ailments.
taxonomy: Dendranthema yoshinaganthum is classified in the family Asteraceae, which is one of the largest and most widespread families of flowering plants. The genus Dendranthema is a group of flowering plants that are native to East Asia. However, some sources may group Dendranthema yoshinaganthum under the genus Chrysanthemum.
